error.signature: RuntimeError: expected scalar type Half but found Float
error.regex: (expected scalar type Half.*found Float|expected.*Float.*found.*Half|dtype mismatch.*half.*float|mixed precision.*type.*error)

verdict.summary: Mixed precision training produces tensors with mismatched dtypes (float16 vs float32). Usually a model or loss function not wrapped in autocast.

dead_ends.action: Cast all tensors to float16 manually
dead_ends.why_fails: Loss computation and gradient accumulation in float16 causes numerical instability and NaN gradients
Loses 2x memory savings and training speed; not necessary if autocast is used correctly

workarounds.how: with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(): output = model(input); loss = criterion(output, target)

workarounds.action: Keep loss computation outside autocast for numerical stability
workarounds.how: Loss scaling and gradient computation should stay in float32

workarounds.action: Use GradScaler to handle float16 gradient underflow
